name: aspell
title: aspell
description:
GNU Aspell spell-checker
GNU Aspell is a spell-checker which can be used either as a standalone
application or embedded in other programs.  Its main feature is that it
does a much better job of suggesting possible spellings than just about
any other spell-checker available for the English language, including
Ispell and Microsoft Word.  It also has many other technical
enhancements over Ispell such as using shared memory for dictionaries
and intelligently handling personal dictionaries when more than one
Aspell process is open at once.
.
Aspell is designed to be a drop-in replacement for Ispell.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use aspell because GNU Aspell spell-checker.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use aspell as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `aspell [options] <command>` as documented upstream for aspell.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair aspell with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `aspell --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install aspell (0.60.8.2-3). Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `aspell [options] <command>`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: GNU Aspell spell-checker.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: GNU Aspell spell-checker.
